body:
Bill - welcome aboard. I know the EES to ETS transition is still fresh, and I want to get ahead of a few items before they create confusion.

I'm told there are three regulatory matters that were active on your desk at EES that are still in flight. I need to understand which of those come with you to ETS and which should stay with EES counsel. This affects both capacity planning and what we're tracking on the FERC side.

Can you pull together a quick summary of the three matters - current status, next steps, and your recommendation on whether they should transfer to ETS ownership or remain at EES? I'd also suggest we loop in whoever the EES counterpart is on their end, so there's no gap in coverage.

Let's aim to have this sorted by end of week. Let me know if you need anything from my end to get oriented. df

Drew - will do. Here's the rundown on the three in-flight items:

**1. EES Affiliate Marketing Compliance Review (FERC)**
Status: Mid-review; we responded to staff data request on 10/12. Waiting on follow-up from FERC on two interrogatories.
Recommendation: Transfer to ETS. The compliance obligations touch on affiliate rules that overlap with TW and NNG operations, and you have better context on the pipeline side.
EES counterpart: Still need to confirm - I'll reach out to Janet Lacy to coordinate.

**2. EES Rate Case Support - Docket No. RP01-___**
Status: Testimony filed 10/5. Settlement conference scheduled for 11/2. I provided supporting analysis but primary advocacy is with outside counsel (Vinson & Elkins).
Recommendation: Remain at EES for now. My role was limited to supporting analysis, and the substantive rate issues are EES-specific. I can stay available for questions but shouldn't own this.
EES counterpart: Janet Lacy is the lead on their end.

**3. EES Capacity Release Audit (FERC)**
Status: Initial response submitted; FERC issued additional document request on 10/18. Response due 11/1.
Recommendation: Transfer to ETS. The capacity release issues implicate pipeline contract interpretation and tariff questions that are squarely in ETS's wheelhouse. I can handle the response with your oversight.
EES counterpart: Again, I'll loop in Janet on handoff.

I'll contact Janet Lacy today to confirm the EES side of these and get her input on the proposed splits. Happy to walk through any of this if you want to discuss before end of week.

BR

Bill - solid breakdown. Your recommendations align with how I'd see these shaking out.

On #1 and #3, I'm comfortable with the transfer to ETS. We'll own those going forward. Coordinate with Janet on the handoff docs and make sure we get confirmation in writing from EES that they're releasing responsibility - no gaps in coverage.

On #2, agreed - that stays with EES. Your supporting role is fine but ownership stays with Janet's team. No need to stay on the hook for it.

Once you hear back from Janet, send me a quick note confirming she's aligned on the splits. If there's anything that needs my sign-off on the EES side, let me know and I'll reach out directly.

Good work getting this sorted quickly. df

Got it. I'll reach out to Janet today and get the handoff documentation squared away. Will send you confirmation once she's aligned on the splits. If anything on the EES side needs your sign-off, I'll flag it. BR
